retrieved_docs.title: Suicidal Ideation -- Etiology -- Ideation-to-action framework
retrieved_docs.content: Interpersonal theory of suicide: As per the interpersonal theory of suicide, 'acquired capability' is a critical factor in the progression from suicidal ideation to suicide attempts. This capability is marked by a decreased fear of death and a heightened tolerance for physical pain resulting from repeated exposure to painful or distressing events, such as childhood trauma or war. An individual with an elevated level of acquired capability is more prone to transition from suicide ideation to suicide attempts.

retrieved_docs.title: Suicide Risk -- Evaluation -- Potential for Homicide
retrieved_docs.content: A mixed-methods study in the UK analyzed 60 homicide-suicides and found that most victims were spouses/partners and/or children. Most perpetrators were male (88%), and most victims were female (77%). Few perpetrators had been in recent contact with mental health services before the incident (12%).

retrieved_docs.title: Suicidal Ideation -- History and Physical -- Assessment of Suicidal Ideations
retrieved_docs.content: Access to means: Evaluating access to lethal means is essential, as increased access raises the risk of a deadly suicide attempt.

retrieved_docs.title: Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: The completion of the MMPI holds value in determining care throughout a variety of treatment considerations. The test should be administered by a licensed psychotherapist, usually a psychiatrist or clinical psychologist, with informed consent obtained by discussing the risks and benefits of completion. Analysis of the results by the psychotherapist interpreting scoring should be attached with a working diagnosis to assess for treatment response. The presence of conditions associated with high-scoring categories will ultimately guide the necessity for pharmacological or non-pharmacological treatment options. This will, in turn, outline the need for referral to appropriate mental healthcare, from continuing outpatient follow-up to institutionalization with fully-staffed nursing and rehabilitative care. Transfer of care should involve appropriate discussion of MMPI data correlated with a summary of interventions. High-scoring in concerning scales such as suicidality highlights an existing need for acute observation or placement. The validity of symptoms should also be corroborated to demonstrate whether a patient is malingering or suffering from organic disorders requiring medical management by a treatment team. The bio-ethical implications of the MMPI should also be identified if the patient is completing the test in concurrence or stipulation with legal charges, and they should be counseled on what findings might hold concerning criminality. The basis for the use of test data in determining adherence has also been documented in a sample consisting of 471 psychiatric patients, with externalizing scales predictive of whether a patient will be more likely to terminate treatment. [1] [Level 3] This illustrates the need for multi-level involvement in facilitating outreach and patient compliance.

retrieved_docs.content: Over the years, several other factors have been identified that increases the risk of suicide, and they include: Major childhood adverse events, for example, sexual abuse Discriminated for being gay, lesbian, transgender, or bisexual Having access to lethal means A long history of being bullied Chronic sleep problems

retrieved_docs.content: The Thesaurus of Psychological Index Terms is organized under the broad injury concept (إصابة) and includes the entry “رفض المريض للعلاج” (patient refusal of treatment). Within this hierarchy, “Antemortem injury” maps to “Unspecified site injury,” which has a child category “Circumstances Related to Injury or Death” that branches into “الاعتداء” (assault) and “تشويه الذات” (self‑harm). “تشويه الذات” is linked to “جرح” (wound), indicating that self‑harm often results in wound injuries. “Unspecified site injury” is related to “intracranial injury” and is primarily mapped from the Arabic phrase “إصابات متعددة” (multiple injuries). “Intracranial injury” is classified as “إصابة داخل الجمجمة” (injury inside the skull) and is associated with the anatomical site “hlava” (head). This category also serves as the parent for “Poisoning / injury,” reinforcing the connection between toxic and traumatic mechanisms. Specific diagnoses under “إصابة داخل الجمجمة” include “Fracture of anterior fossa,”

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 820
retrieved_docs.content: The category “Circumstances Related to Injury or Death” encompasses both “الاعتداء” (assault) and “تشويه الذات” (self‑harm). Within this framework, “تشويه الذات” is linked to “جرح” (wound), indicating that self‑harm commonly results in physical injuries. Recognizing assault and self‑harm as distinct but related subcategories under circumstances of injury or death, and understanding the direct relationship between self‑harm and wound formation, aids clinicians in accurately classifying incidents, assessing risk, and planning appropriate medical and psychosocial interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 1 -- Community 181
retrieved_docs.content: The knowledge base arranges toxic‑exposure, injury, mental‑health sequelae and adverse medical‑care events in a multi‑layered hierarchy that links broad categories to specific agents, clinical presentations and contextual risk factors. At the apex, the Arabic category «سمية لمختلف العوامل» (toxicity of various factors) and the English category «POISONING/INJURY» encompass all toxic events, while parallel domains such as «e‑codes: adverse effects of medical care», «suicide and intentional self‑inflicted injury», «اضطراب الجهاز المناعي» (immune system disorder) and «التهاب الدماغ الفيروسي المنقول بالمفصليات» (viral encephalitis transmitted by arthropods) are positioned as complementary concepts. Within the toxic‑exposure domain, «POISONING BY DRUGS AND BIOLOGICALS» is placed under the Arabic umbrella «تسمم بمؤثرات عقلية غير محددة» (poisoning by unspecified mental‑effect agents) and serves as the parent of «POISONING BY DRUGS, MEDICINAL AND BIOLOGICAL SUBSTANCES», which in turn is a child

retrieved_docs.content: linked to the solitary, aggressive‑type behavior disorder, positioning attentional difficulties opposite oppositional defiant disorder. Childhood‑onset disorders are related to unspecified active childhood psychosis, a member of the psychosis and psychotic disorders (SMQ) group. Depression excluding suicide and self‑harm (الاكتئاب (باستثناء الانتحار وإيذاء النفس) (SMQ)) aggregates a broad spectrum of depressive subtypes—including recurrent and single‑episode major depressive disorder (in full or complete remission), reactive, endogenous, melancholic, atypical, and obsessive‑type depression—as well as substance‑use disorder, reflecting frequent comorbidity with addictive behaviors. This SMQ depression construct also includes transient and continuous disorders of awakening onset/maintenance and habituation and is classified within post‑natal depression (excluding psychosis). Associated features extend beyond affective symptoms to mood lability, emotional poverty, indifference,

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 298
retrieved_docs.content: Disorder due to and following poisoning is a broad diagnostic category that encompasses both التسمم (poisoning) and poisoning by nonmedicinal substances as specific subtypes. Within this framework, التسمم is also classified under the category of suicide and intentional self‑inflicted injury, highlighting that poisoning is a recognized method of self‑harm. Moreover, التسمم is associated with تسمم بالأدوية المؤشرة على انحلال الليفين (a form of drug‑induced poisoning) and with متلازمة الأدرينالية (adrenal syndrome), indicating that poisoning can co‑occur with or contribute to these particular clinical conditions. This integrated view underscores the interrelationship between general poisoning disorders, specific toxic exposures, self‑inflicted injury, and related systemic syndromes, which is essential for accurate diagnosis, risk assessment, and comprehensive patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 3 -- Community 2538
retrieved_docs.content: The Thesaurus of Psychological Index Terms is organized with the concept “رفض المريض للعلاج” (patient’s refusal of treatment) listed as a child term, indicating that this specific psychological index term is included within the Thesaurus. At the same time, the Thesaurus of Psychological Index Terms is positioned under the broader parent category “إصابة” (injury), linking the collection of psychological terms to the general concept of injury. Consequently, patient refusal of treatment is understood as a psychological factor related to injury, emphasizing its importance for clinicians who must consider the possibility that individuals with injuries may decline recommended therapeutic interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 27 -- Level 4 -- Community 2754
retrieved_docs.content: Psychological disorder (اضطراب نفسي) includes depression (اكتئاب) as a child condition, indicating that depression is a specific subtype within the broader category of mental health disorders. The concept of an “Allowed qualifier” connects the attribute of mortality (mortalita) to both psychological disorder and depression, signifying that mortality is recognized as a permissible and relevant outcome measure for evaluating these conditions. Recognizing depression as a child of psychological disorder and acknowledging mortality as an allowed qualifier for both aids clinicians in comprehensive risk assessment and informed patient care planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2645
retrieved_docs.content: The classification “Circumstances Related to Injury or Death” includes a range of contextual categories that describe how harmful events occur, and one of its specific child concepts is “الاعتداء,” which denotes an assault or violent attack. Identifying “الاعتداء” as a distinct circumstance within this broader category aids clinicians, forensic investigators, and public‑health professionals in accurately documenting, analyzing, and responding to injuries or fatalities that result from intentional violence.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 35
retrieved_docs.content: Adolescent mental disorder is defined as a pathological developmental process that is linked to a solitary‑aggressive type behavior disorder and may manifest as isolated or aggressive behavioral patterns. Within this framework, “mental symptom” is associated with transient organic psychotic states (حالات ذُهانية عضوية عابرة), a subcategory of organic psychosis (ذُهان عضوي), and includes child‑specific concepts such as problem‑personality (probleem; persoonlijkheid), loss of pleasure (فقد المتعة), and self‑destructive behavior (السلوك التدميري الذاتي). Transient organic psychotic states comprise non‑infectious encephalitis (التهاب الدماغ غير المعدي (SMQ)), psychosis and psychotic disorders (الذهان والاضطرابات الذهانية (SMQ)), and delirium/organic brain disease (الهذيان/الاعتلال الدماغي غير المعدي (SMQ)). retrieved_docs.content: (heroin addiction) is further classified as a form of الاعتماد على الأدوية (drug dependence), linking substance‑use disorders to the broader dependence framework. Disorder due to and following poisoning is a clinical category that is inversely linked to التسمم (poisoning), representing conditions that arise after any of the toxic events described above. التسمم serves as a parent for numerous more detailed classifications: Suicide and intentional self‑inflicted injury is a child of التسمم, and Poisoning by analgesic drug is specifically related to these self‑harm behaviors. Analgesic drug poisoning is also identified as a form of ketamine poisoning and, through an inverse‑isa relationship, belongs to the wider group التسمم بالعقاقير (drug poisoning). Poisonings and Drug Effects is a subcategory of التسمم and is linked to الاعتماد على الأدوية (drug dependence). retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 24 -- Level 1 -- Community 55
retrieved_docs.content: is dual‑classified as both poisoning caused by a biological substance and poisoning due to a chemical substance. Poisoning by analgesic drugs is associated with suicide and intentional self‑inflicted injury, highlighting its relevance to mental‑health risk assessment. The Arabic term for drug poisoning (التسمم بالعقاقير) is a subclass of the unspecified psychoactive‑substance poisoning group, reinforcing that drug‑related toxicities are part of a larger psychoactive toxicity spectrum. Poisoning by other medications and drugs is further linked to the “late effect of poisoning due to drug, medicinal, or biological substance,” emphasizing the importance of recognizing delayed toxic sequelae. Terminology from other languages (e.g., “intoxicatie; anxiolyticum,” “opioïden; intoxicatie”) maps to the concept of drug abuse (تعاطي مخدرات), underscoring the clinical relevance of substance‑use contexts in toxicology. Overall, the knowledge base integrates specific toxic agents, their parent drug
